generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR sensor providing raw point cloud data for environmental perception in autonomous driving scenarios. Captures 2D planar point clouds for object detection and tracking."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance IntelRealSenseD435iCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel RealSense D435i depth camera capturing RGB-D data for environmental perception, including ego vehicle state and obstacle detection in autonomous driving applications."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CameraDepthData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Onboard sensor suite capturing vehicle dynamics metrics including speed, position, orientation, steering angle, and motor/servo states for real-time autonomous control."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateData
]

instance MotorActuatorFeedbackSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor monitoring motor and servo actuation feedback (e.g., voltage commands, steering angles) to ensure synchronized control between physical and digital twin systems."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ActuationFeedbackData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point cloud data transmitted from Hokuyo UST-10LX sensor, representing 2D planar points for environmental perception."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
]

instance CameraDepthData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "RGB-D data from Intel RealSense D435i camera, including depth information for object detection and vehicle state estimation."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelRealSenseD435iCamera
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Processed vehicle state metrics (speed, position, orientation, steering angle) transmitted for real-time autonomous control and synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance ActuationFeedbackData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Motor and servo actuation feedback data (voltage commands, steering angles) transmitted for closed-loop control validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorActuatorFeedbackSensor
]

instance ProcessedObjectTracks : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Structured object tracks derived from raw LiDAR and camera data via Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking, including lead vehicle detection and obstacle identification."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR, IntelRealSenseD435iCamera
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Software component processing raw LiDAR point clouds into structured object tracks (e.g., lead vehicle detection) using clustering and tracking algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateDataModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model standardizing and formatting vehicle state data (speed, position, orientation) for transmission to the Digital Twin Entity."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler
]

instance LiDARPointCloudDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point cloud data collected from Hokuyo UST-10LX sensor, representing 2D planar points for environmental perception."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler
]

instance CameraDepthDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "RGB-D data from Intel RealSense D435i camera, including depth information for object detection and vehicle state estimation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CameraDepthData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler
]

instance ProcessedVehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Standardized vehicle state metrics (speed, position, orientation, steering angle) formatted for real-time autonomous control."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler
]

instance ProcessedObjectTracksData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured object tracks (e.g., lead vehicle, obstacles) derived from raw sensor data via Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProcessedObjectTracks
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance ActuationFeedbackDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Motor and servo actuation feedback data (voltage commands, steering angles) for closed-loop control validation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ActuationFeedbackData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance SQLDatabaseStoredData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured data (object tracks, vehicle state) stored in SQL database for reporting, visualization, and post-mortem analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProcessedObjectTracksData, ProcessedVehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataStorageEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance DataPreProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional Entity standardizing and formatting raw sensor data (LiDAR, camera, vehicle state) before transmission to the Digital Twin Entity."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional Entity serializing, aligning, and converting data between message types and unit systems for inter-entity communication."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance DataStorageEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional Entity managing structured data storage in SQL database for reporting, visualization, and historical analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance WiFiCommunicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Wi-Fi network facilitating low-latency, high-bandwidth communication between Physical Twin and Digital Twin Entities (implied UDP transport)."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance ROS2DDSCommunicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 DDS middleware enabling decentralized, distributed communication between Functional Entities with temporal synchronization (100ms)."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization, data logging, and reporting service for operators and developers, displaying live vehicle state and object tracks."
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleStateVisualizationInsight, ObjectTrackVisualizationInsight, DataLoggingAction
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Distributed computing service offloading ACC logic from onboard ECUs to Digital Twin Entity, ensuring sub-100ms latency for safety-critical operations."
    DTDFVocab:provides LeadVehicleDetectionInsight, DesiredSpeedCalculationInsight, ActuationCommandRelayAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Teleoperation service enabling remote ACC control, emergency intervention, and geographical decoupling of development/operation."
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCControlInterfaceInsight, EmergencyBrakingAction, SystemOverrideAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance VehicleStateVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of ego vehicle state (speed, acceleration, sensor inputs) and projected driving paths for situational awareness."
]

instance ObjectTrackVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "3D visualization of detected objects (lead vehicle, obstacles) derived from LiDAR and camera data for monitoring and validation."
]

instance DataLoggingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated logging of vehicle states, sensor inputs, and control actions for post-mortem analysis and report generation."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance LeadVehicleDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification and tracking of lead vehicle using sensor fusion (radar, camera, LiDAR) for ACC decision-making."
]

instance DesiredSpeedCalculationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Computation of adaptive speed based on distance-to-lead-vehicle and safety margins (time-headway, deceleration rates)."
]

instance ActuationCommandRelayAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Transmission of computed desired speed to physical twin for throttle/brake adjustments in closed-loop control."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ACCControlInterfaceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Remote interface for enabling/disabling ACC, setting desired speeds, and issuing emergency commands."
]

instance EmergencyBrakingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote emergency braking command execution with fail-safes for latency mitigation."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SystemOverrideAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Manual override capability for operators to intervene in ACC operations remotely."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance VedderElectronicSpeedController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuator converting desired speed inputs into motor voltage commands for propulsion control in autonomous driving scenarios."
]

instance SteeringActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuator executing steering angle commands derived from ACC logic for vehicle trajectory control."
]

// C1: Physical Twin System
instance AutonomousVehiclePhysicalTwin : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "1/10-scale autonomous vehicle retrofitted with advanced sensor and control technology for Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) applications."
]

instance LowerLevelChassis : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Foundational mechanical and power systems (propulsion, steering, braking) supporting autonomous operation in the physical twin."
    base:contains VedderElectronicSpeedController, SteeringActuator
]

instance UpperLevelChassis : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Autonomy elements including embedded ECUs, sensor fusion modules, and control logic for decision-making and actuation."
    base:contains HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR, IntelRealSenseD435iCamera, VehicleStateSensor, MotorActuatorFeedbackSensor
]

instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical Twin Entity hosting resource-specific functionality (sensors, actuators, Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking) for autonomous vehicle operation."
    base:contains LowerLevelChassis, UpperLevelChassis
]

instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Digital Twin Entity managing data pre-processing, actuation, and device communication as the central coordination layer for twinning logic."
    base:contains DataPreProcessingEnabler, DataTranslationEnabler, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance UserEntity : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "User Entity containing the User Interface Functional Entity split into 3D Visualization and Control Panel subcomponents."
    base:contains DataMonitoringService
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **Virtual-to-Physical Interaction** in the Digital Twin System (DTS) involves control commands generated by the **Digital Twin Entity** and transmitted to the **Physical Twin Entity** via the following workflow:

1. **Command Types & Control Flows**:
   - **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) Commands**: The **Controlling FE** (ACC) determines the lead vehicle and computes a following speed, which is translated into control signals.
   - **Actuation Commands**: The **Actuation FE** converts desired speed into **motor voltage commands** and steering servo actuation signals.

2. **Control Message Formats**:
   - Commands are serialized and aligned via the **Data Translation** component (ISO 23247 Data Translation FE), ensuring compatibility with the **Physical Twin Entity**’s input requirements.
   - No explicit mention of message formats (e.g., ROS2 message types like `std_msgs`), but commands are propagated through the system’s software components.

3. **Validation & Authentication Mechanisms**:
   - Not explicitly detailed in the documents. Implicit validation may occur via the **Data Translation** component (e.g., unit conversion checks) and the **Actuation FE** (e.g., ensuring motor voltage commands are within operational limits).

4. **Feedback Systems**:
   - The **Physical Twin Entity** provides real-time feedback (e.g., motor state, steering position) back to the **Digital Twin Entity** via sensor data loops (e.g., LiDAR, vehicle state sensors).
   - No explicit mention of conflict resolution (e.g., priority-based arbitration) or command execution confirmation (e.g., acknowledgment signals).

5. **User Interaction**:
   - The **User Entity** interacts with the system via the **Control Panel** (User Interface FE), which allows manual overrides or adjustments to ACC parameters (e.g., desired speed, safety thresholds).
   - User inputs are processed by the **Controlling FE** and translated into control commands for the **Actuation FE**.

6. **Execution Workflow**:
   - Commands flow from the **Digital Twin Entity** → **Controlling FE** → **Actuation FE** → **Physical Twin Entity** (e.g., motor, steering servo).
   - No explicit mention of latency requirements for control loops, but real-time operation is implied for ACC functionality.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) enforces a **100 ms clock synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination, adhering to hardware limitations. This temporal granularity ensures deterministic behavior for safety-critical vehicular applications, where stricter synchronization (e.g., sub-100 ms) is mandated by many standards but not implemented here. The system does not explicitly specify distinct time scales for different services, but the **ROS2-based architecture** (with each Functional Entity (FE) implemented as a ROS2 package) implies real-time constraints for inter-node communication, likely leveraging ROS2's **DDS (Data Distribution Service) middleware** for low-latency, publish-subscribe interactions. No explicit latency requirements beyond synchronization are documented, though the **Physical Twin Entity** (hosting sensors/actuators) and **Digital Twin Entity** (data management/control) must operate within this 100 ms window for coherent twinning. The **User Entity** (3D Visualization/Control Panel) may tolerate slightly higher latency but remains dependent on the synchronized data pipeline."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DTS follows an **entity-centric orchestration pattern** rooted in the ISO 23247 RA, where software components are mapped to Functional Entities (FEs) and deployed as **individual ROS2 packages** (one per FE). Each package contains one or more ROS2 nodes implementing the FE’s functionality, enabling modular execution on heterogeneous hardware. The **Physical Twin Entity**, **Digital Twin Entity**, and **User Entity** are deployed on separate hardware components, introducing **substantial deviations from the standard** due to ownership and engineering process implications. Resource management is implicit within each entity’s scope: the Physical Twin Entity prioritizes real-time sensor/actuator data processing (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*), while the Digital Twin Entity manages data abstraction and control logic. Inter-entity communication occurs over a **Wi-Fi network**, adhering to a **standardized communication interface** (ROS2-based) without explicit mention of DDS QoS policies or dynamic discovery mechanisms. The architecture avoids redundant FEs, excluding unnecessary ISO 23247 components while splitting the *User Interface FE* into visualization and control subcomponents."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The ISO 23247 standard does not explicitly detail **fidelity requirements** (e.g., resolution, granularity, or abstraction levels) for the Digital Twin Entity or its models. However, the framework implies **validation and verification** through the **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**, which ensures data accuracy and consistency between the physical OME and its digital representation. Uncertainty sources (e.g., sensor noise, model approximations) are not quantified, nor are error detection/correction mechanisms specified. The standard lacks explicit **quality assurance processes** for models or data, though the modular **Functional Entities (FEs)** suggest potential for localized validation (e.g., actuating FEs ensuring control commands align with physical constraints). The absence of guidelines on implementation (e.g., OPC UA) further limits fidelity considerations to user-defined specifications."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin System (DTS) Technical Connection** relies on a hybrid network infrastructure with the following specifications:

1. **Communication Protocols**:
   - **Intra-Entity (Software Components)**: Communication occurs within a **ROS2 network**, enabling modular and distributed software components (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking, Data Translation, Controlling FE) to exchange data via publish-subscribe or service-based interactions.
   - **Inter-Entity (Physical ↔ Digital Twin)**: Communication between the **Physical Twin Entity**, **Digital Twin Entity**, and **User Entity** occurs over a **Wi-Fi network** (transport layer partially specified as UDP in the context).

2. **Network Topology & Architecture**:
   - **Modular Deployment**: Each Functional Entity (FE) is implemented as an individual **ROS2 package**, allowing for scalable and independent deployment.
   - **Entity Separation**: The **Physical Twin Entity** (sensors/actuators), **Digital Twin Entity** (processing/visualization), and **User Entity** (interface) are distinct but interconnected via the described network layers.

3. **Latency Requirements**:
   - The system must maintain a **network latency of at most 100 ms** to ensure real-time operation for ACC and visualization purposes.
   - No explicit mention of bandwidth requirements or Quality of Service (QoS) policies, but real-time constraints imply low-latency prioritization.

4. **Reliability & Redundancy Mechanisms**:
   - Not explicitly detailed in the documents. Implicit reliability may be achieved through:
     - **ROS2’s built-in fault tolerance** (e.g., retry mechanisms for failed service calls).
     - **Wi-Fi redundancy** (if multiple access points are used, though not specified).
   - No mention of failover strategies or hardware redundancy.

5. **Security Protocols**:
   - Not explicitly described in the provided documents. Security measures (e.g., encryption, authentication) are not mentioned for network communication.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin System's hosting and deployment infrastructure** is distributed across **Physical Twin Entity**, **Digital Twin Entity**, and **User Entity** components, each deployed on distinct hardware. The **Physical Twin Entity** includes **LiDAR sensors**, **motor controllers**, and **steering servos**, while the **Digital Twin Entity** hosts software components like the **Data Pre-Processing FE**, **Controlling FE**, and **Data Translation FE**, but the document does **not specify the exact hosting platform** (e.g., cloud, edge, or on-premise) for these components. However, the **User Entity** (e.g., **3D Visualization UI**, **Control Panel**) is likely deployed on a **local workstation** or **embedded system** (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson NX/Orin Nano for the Physical Twin, as mentioned in the architecture but not explicitly for the Digital Twin). **Computational requirements** are implied by the need for real-time processing (e.g., point cloud clustering, Kalman filtering), suggesting edge deployment for low-latency operations. **Storage requirements** are met via a **centralized database** (e.g., SQL) for structured data, while **scalability mechanisms** are not detailed but may involve **modular ROS2 nodes** or **cloud-offloaded analytics**. **Availability/reliability assurance** is not explicitly described, though the use of **ROS2 QoS policies** and **Wi-Fi redundancy** (if applicable) may contribute to robustness. The document does **not confirm cloud/edge/on-premise hosting** for the Digital Twin Entity, so this remains ambiguous."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to the **ISO 23247 standard**, specifically **Part 2: Reference Architecture**, which defines an **entity-based framework** for manufacturing Digital Twins. Key compliance aspects include:
  - **Entity-based Reference Architecture (RA)**: Structured around **Observable Manufacturing Elements (OMEs)**, **Data Collection and Device Control Entities**, **Digital Twin Entities**, and **User Entities**, each with modular **Functional Entities (FEs)**.
  - **Interoperability Standards**: The standard does not mandate specific protocols but permits technologies like **OPC Unified Architecture** for data exchange between entities.
  - **Certification and Compliance Frameworks**: No explicit certification requirements are mentioned, but adherence to **ISO 23247** ensures alignment with manufacturing Digital Twin best practices.
  - **Information Exchange**: Part 4 of the standard (not detailed here) governs data exchange, implying compliance with **industry-specific interoperability standards** for seamless integration across manufacturing systems."
]
